overview: The Marathon County Environmental Resources Committee approved two rezoning petitions unanimously and voted to forward a modified fee waiver resolution to the county board that will only waive reconnect fees for septic systems damaged by the April 17, 2026 tornado, rejecting broader fee waivers. The committee also received educational presentations from Solid Waste, Parks Recreation and Forestry, and Conservation Planning and Zoning departments.

agenda.item: Call to order and Pledge of Allegiance
Public hearing - Tim Veland rezoning petition (urban residential/general agriculture to rural residential, Town of Green Valley)
Public hearing - Jeffrey and Cynthia Schmidtz rezoning petition (Conservancy Recreation to General Agriculture, Town of McMillan)
Consideration of fee waiver for properties impacted by April 17, 2026 severe weather
Educational presentation - Solid Waste Department overview
Educational presentation - Parks, Recreation, and Forestry Department overview
Educational presentation - Conservation, Planning, and Zoning Department overview

discussions.body: Supervisor Sefeld made a motion to approve the minutes, seconded by Supervisor Drebeck. The motion carried with no opposition.

discussions.item: Tim Veland rezoning petition - Town of Green Valley
discussions.body: Land Use Specialist Garrett Pogle presented the staff report for rezoning from urban residential and general agriculture to rural residential. The Town of Green Valley recommended approval on March 10, 2026. No public testimony was offered for, against, or as interested. Supervisor Drebeck moved to approve based on findings of fact and conclusions of law, seconded by Supervisor Kurt. Motion carried unanimously.

discussions.item: Jeffrey and Cynthia Schmidtz rezoning petition - Town of McMillan
discussions.body: Garrett Pogle presented the petition to rezone from Conservancy Recreation to General Agriculture. Supervisor Leur asked about the conservancy nature of the property; Pogle explained it currently has a home and horse stable, with the rezoning needed to make the home a conforming use. He confirmed no natural area would be lost. The Town of McMillan recommended approval on April 20, 2026. No public testimony was offered. Supervisor Sefeld moved to approve, seconded by Supervisor Drew. Motion carried unanimously.

Director Lori Mskimmons presented a proposal to waive county zoning and septic fees for properties damaged by the April 17, 2026 tornado. Supervisor Drew questioned where to draw the line for future events. Supervisor Leur asked about tracking costs. Supervisor Drebeck expressed concern about fairness to those not in declared disaster areas and referenced the recent POT fee increase. After discussion, Supervisor Sefeld moved to forward the resolution. Supervisor Drebeck then proposed an amendment to limit the waiver only to reconnect fees for septic systems (not full system replacements or zoning fees). The amendment passed, then the amended resolution was approved with one opposing vote to forward to the county board.

Director Dave Hagen Busher presented an educational overview covering the landfill's history since 1980, the renewable natural gas partnership with Vidi Energy generating royalties since 2023, leachate treatment challenges due to PFAS regulations requiring on-site treatment by 2032, and upcoming $6 million expansion projects. He noted the landfill accepts about 1,000 tons daily.

discussions.item: Parks, Recreation, and Forestry Department overview
discussions.body: County Forest Administrator Tom Lovelin presented on behalf of Director Jamie, covering the joint city-county department structure with 44 full-time and 120 seasonal employees. He highlighted Nine Mile County Forest generating $3.25 million in economic impact from out-of-town visitors. Supervisor Sefeld noted Tom Lovelin is retiring June 1st after 35 years. Chair Ritterder thanked him for his service.

actionItems: Forward amended fee waiver resolution to county board - waiving only reconnect fees for septic systems on tornado-damaged properties for one year
Forward Tim Veland rezoning petition (Green Valley) to county board for approval
Forward Jeffrey and Cynthia Schmidtz rezoning petition (McMillan) to county board for approval
Joe Tucker selected as new County Forest Administrator replacing retiring Tom Lovelin
title: Marathon County Environmental Resources Committee Meeting
